artPrompt: A widescreen noir film-still of a goblin don leaning intimately close to a cornered figure in a dim back room, one clawed hand resting on the man's shoulder, lips at his ear, the threat unspoken; a single amber lamp carves their two faces from ink-black shadow, cigar smoke hanging heavy, a worn card table and bottle in soft focus, suffocating quiet menace, rendered as gritty cinematic digital concept art — highly detailed, dramatic photoreal lighting, atmospheric haze, realistic textures.
name: Make Him an Offer
text:
Choose target opponent and lean in close. Make them an offer they can't refuse: you say, out loud, in your best gravelly voice, "give me the [permanent], or [consequence]." Sacrifice a Favor token. That player gives you control of target permanent they control.
If you didn't do the voice, the offer stands anyway, but everyone agrees you missed an opportunity.
flavorText: He made an offer. It was, technically, refusable. He had simply never met anyone who tried.
